status: first-pass evidence bundle exported from tracker rows

observations.outcome_type: sensory axon growth initiation and elongation
observations.observation_value: Electrical stimulation/depolarization enhanced sensory axon growth initiation after SCI but was less robust than conditioning lesion for elongation through the lesion.
observations.normalized_value: ES enhances initiation more than elongation
observations.statistics_reported: Full-text first pass; exact growth distances not materialized here
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Sciatic nerve stimulation produced frequency-dependent dorsal horn metabolic activation but did not increase DRG cell-body glucose utilization.
observations.normalized_value: sciatic ES activates spinal terminals not DRG soma metabolism
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.outcome_type: DRG neurite outgrowth and peripheral nerve repair
observations.observation_value: Brief ES can enhance DRG neurite outgrowth and peripheral nerve repair outcomes, but conditioning ES and lesion conditioning are not equivalent across endpoints.
observations.normalized_value: brief ES supports PNS repair and DRG neurites
observations.statistics_reported: Review reports 20 Hz for 1 h intact sciatic ES enhanced DRG neurite extension
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Review states ES activates overlapping pathways with peripheral conditioning lesions but does not fully recapitulate conditioning lesion effects.
observations.normalized_value: ES overlaps but does not equal lesion conditioning
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Peripheral lesion preconditioning is the benchmark for central sensory axon growth beyond dorsal column lesion.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Lesion conditioning recruited broader and longer-lasting genetic programs than cAMP, setting a benchmark for ES mimicry.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: STAT3 activation is a required DRG growth-state benchmark for conditioning-like stimulation approaches.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Enriched conditioning shows activity-state synergy with lesion conditioning through PKC-STAT3-NOX2 signaling.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Peripheral conditioning effects can be branch-specific and cannot be assumed to improve central branch repair in every assay.
observations.statistics_reported: Peripheral branch increased 4.4 to 5.5 mm/day; central branch not significantly affected
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Conditioning lesions alter neurite initiation, growth rate, and branching, defining phenotype dimensions for ES comparison.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Lesion conditioning can produce remote/non-associated DRG priming; ES needs separate evidence before remote effects are inferred.
observations.normalized_value: remote DRG priming not assumed for ES
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Conditioning-like claims must preserve sequence and timing because reversed-order effects differ from preconditioning.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Conditioning effects on regenerated axon numbers require sufficient interval between lesions; timing is a stimulation-design boundary.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: AMPK controls DRG sensory-neuron regenerative ability, supporting metabolic readouts for ES preconditioning.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Dorsal column model defines the assay boundary for testing whether ES improves central branch regeneration.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

observations.observation_value: Even regenerated sensory afferents after conditioning-plus-repair can remain chronically pathophysiological, setting functional safety boundaries for ES-enabled repair.
observations.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: PNS electrical stimulation can enhance sensory axon growth initiation after SCI, but current evidence suggests it is less robust than conditioning lesion for sustained elongation through CNS lesions.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Sciatic nerve stimulation can activate spinal afferent-terminal metabolism without necessarily activating DRG soma metabolism.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Brief PNS electrical stimulation has strong peripheral nerve repair and DRG neurite evidence, but CNS repair claims need separate central-branch testing.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Electrical stimulation overlaps with conditioning lesion molecular pathways but should not be treated as fully equivalent to crush or axotomy conditioning.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Peripheral axotomy preconditioning remains the benchmark for central sensory axon growth beyond dorsal column lesion.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Conditioning lesion recruits broader gene-expression mechanisms than cAMP, so ES should be evaluated against broad growth-state readouts rather than a single pathway marker.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: STAT3 activation is a useful benchmark readout for DRG conditioning-like effects.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Activity-state and redox pathways may synergize with conditioning, so ES experiments should assay more than electrical activation alone.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Branch-specific conditioning evidence warns against assuming peripheral nerve growth effects automatically translate to central branch regeneration.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: ES studies should distinguish neurite initiation, outgrowth rate, elongation, and branching because conditioning lesions affect these dimensions differently.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Remote DRG priming after lesion conditioning is real, but electrical stimulation requires direct evidence before remote or contralateral DRG priming is inferred.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: ES conditioning experiments must preserve sequence and timing because conditioning effects vary when peripheral stimulation or lesioning occurs before versus after CNS injury.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: Conditioning biology is interval-dependent, making stimulation schedule a mechanistic variable rather than simple protocol metadata.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: AMPK and metabolic growth-state pathways are candidate readouts for PNS ES preconditioning studies.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: The dorsal column model is the appropriate central-branch assay for testing whether PNS ES improves DRG sensory axon regeneration after SCI.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

author_claims.claim_text: ES-enabled regeneration strategies must include chronic functional physiology and sensory-pathophysiology safety endpoints.
author_claims.source_section: Module 16A tracker / source-page, full-text PMC, or review metadata

evidence_links.notes: Lesion benchmark contextualizes ES effect size.
Dorsal column model contextualizes afferent pathway.
Direct CNS ES row limits peripheral repair extrapolation.
Broad gene program comparator supports non-equivalence.
ES row compares against lesion benchmark.
Neurite phenotype row supports multi-endpoint readout.
AMPK row supports metabolic readout context.
Dorsal column model supports branch-specific assay boundary.
Brief ES DRG neurite row supports endpoint granularity.
Brief ES row lacks remote DRG proof and supports boundary.
Metabolic stimulation row supports pathway-readout design.
Lesion benchmark supports dorsal column assay choice.

Peripheral repair ES row needs CNS safety separation.
Timing row supports avoiding remote inference without direct schedule/side data.

Chronic physiology row reinforces safety boundary.

boundary_records.boundary_statement: PNS electrical stimulation must be separated from peripheral nerve lesion conditioning because stimulation may only partially mimic lesion-induced DRG growth states.
Stimulation target and nerve status matter: intact nerve, transected stump, repaired nerve, DRG-linked afferent, and dorsal column context are not interchangeable.
Parameters are causal fields: frequency, pulse width, intensity, duration, repetition, and timing relative to injury must be preserved.
Peripheral nerve repair outcomes do not automatically establish CNS sensory axon regeneration after SCI.
ES effects should distinguish regeneration initiation, elongation through lesion, growth beyond lesion, neurite branching, and chronic functional status.
DRG soma, peripheral axon, central axon, spinal terminal, and spinal lesion environment are separate compartments.
Remote or contralateral DRG priming cannot be inferred from local PNS ES without direct side/segment evidence.
Translation requires non-destructive stimulation feasibility plus chronic sensory physiology, pain, and maladaptive plasticity safety readouts.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: PNS electrical stimulation is consensus-ready as a non-destructive conditioning-like strategy that can enhance sensory axon growth initiation, but current evidence does not support treating it as fully equivalent to peripheral nerve crush or axotomy conditioning for sustained central regeneration through SCI lesions.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: PNS electrical stimulation is consensus-ready as a non-destructive conditioning-like strategy that can enhance sensory axon growth initiation, but current evidence does not support treating it as fully equivalent to peripheral nerve crush or axotomy conditioning for sustained central regeneration through SCI lesions.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Sciatic nerve stimulation can activate spinal afferent-terminal metabolism without parallel DRG soma metabolic activation, so PNS ES studies must distinguish peripheral axon, spinal terminal, DRG cell body, and central lesion compartments.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Sciatic nerve stimulation can activate spinal afferent-terminal metabolism without parallel DRG soma metabolic activation, so PNS ES studies must distinguish peripheral axon, spinal terminal, DRG cell body, and central lesion compartments.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Brief electrical stimulation has strong peripheral nerve repair and DRG neurite-outgrowth support, but CNS repair claims require direct central-branch, dorsal-column, lesion-crossing, and chronic functional endpoints.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Brief electrical stimulation has strong peripheral nerve repair and DRG neurite-outgrowth support, but CNS repair claims require direct central-branch, dorsal-column, lesion-crossing, and chronic functional endpoints.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Conditioning lesion biology should be used as the benchmark for ES preconditioning: cAMP, STAT3, transcriptional programs, timing, interval, and branch-specific outcomes define what must be measured before claiming ES has induced a true DRG growth state.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Conditioning lesion biology should be used as the benchmark for ES preconditioning: cAMP, STAT3, transcriptional programs, timing, interval, and branch-specific outcomes define what must be measured before claiming ES has induced a true DRG growth state.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Activity-state and metabolic pathways, including PKC-STAT3-NOX2 redox signaling and AMPK-linked regenerative ability, are candidate readouts for PNS ES preconditioning but need direct perturbation in ES-conditioned SCI models.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Activity-state and metabolic pathways, including PKC-STAT3-NOX2 redox signaling and AMPK-linked regenerative ability, are candidate readouts for PNS ES preconditioning but need direct perturbation in ES-conditioned SCI models.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Remote or contralateral DRG priming is established for unilateral sciatic lesion conditioning, but PNS electrical stimulation should not inherit that claim until experiments directly measure side, segment, associated versus non-associated DRGs, and later CNS repair endpoints.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Remote or contralateral DRG priming is established for unilateral sciatic lesion conditioning, but PNS electrical stimulation should not inherit that claim until experiments directly measure side, segment, associated versus non-associated DRGs, and later CNS repair endpoints.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: ES-enabled regeneration strategies should include chronic sensory physiology and maladaptive plasticity endpoints because regenerating afferents may remain pathophysiological even when anatomical growth is achieved.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: ES-enabled regeneration strategies should include chronic sensory physiology and maladaptive plasticity endpoints because regenerating afferents may remain pathophysiological even when anatomical growth is achieved.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 16 should function as the PNS electrical-stimulation preconditioning layer: candidate studies should specify nerve target, intact or injured nerve status, stimulation parameters, timing, DRG and spinal readouts, lesion model, and whether the endpoint is peripheral repair, central initiation, lesion traversal, growth beyond lesion, or functional recovery.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 16 should function as the PNS electrical-stimulation preconditioning layer: candidate studies should specify nerve target, intact or injured nerve status, stimulation parameters, timing, DRG and spinal readouts, lesion model, and whether the endpoint is peripheral repair, central initiation, lesion traversal, growth beyond lesion, or functional recovery.
